steps.text:
Time to combine everything you've learned. You're going to
create the project folder where you'll build a real web app
in the next lessons.

Note: if your terminal instructions scroll off the screen
at any point, you can scroll up to see them again.

Here's what you'll build:

    ~/claudzooks-project/
    ├── index.html
    ├── style.css
    └── notes.txt

Create the three files. `touch` creates an empty file —
you learned this in Lesson 3.

Open Finder and navigate to your home folder. You should
see "claudzooks-project" right there, and inside it, the
three files you just created. You did that with the terminal!

Now let's add some content. This is the starter code for
a web page:

steps.command: echo "This project was created during Claudzooks Lesson 5." > notes.txt

steps.text:
Now for the exciting part — let's open your web page in
a browser! You should see a page that says "Hello!"

You made a web page using nothing but terminal commands.

Now let's save where your project lives so the next
lessons can find it.
